themes.name: Customer Support & Issue Resolution
themes.description: Users frequently complain about poor customer support, lack of timely resolution, unresponsiveness, and difficulty in contacting support agents.

themes.name: App Functionality & User Experience
themes.description: Reviews highlight issues with app interface, complexity, cluttered design, font size, missing features, and glitches affecting trading and payments.

themes.name: Charges & Fees
themes.description: Users express dissatisfaction with hidden charges, brokerage fees, and unexpected deductions, particularly for US stocks and certain transactions.

themes.name: Account Management & KYC
themes.description: Complaints related to account closure issues, balance refunds, and a complex or lengthy KYC process.

themes.name: Investment Features & Data
themes.description: Positive feedback on data analysis, tracking capabilities, and investment in US stocks, alongside requests for new features like basket investing.

quotes.text: is platform me sabse ghatiya support milta he koy call nehi lagta he sirf ai se cunnect hota he koy resolution nehi milta koy call back nehi hota

The interface is very much cluttered and font size is kind of very bigger. it's good to track india stock, US stocks and Mutual fund all in one place which is good but it doesn't provide rich UI. Please check all your competitors UI like Zerodha, Groww where you feel so much difference. The font is too much bigger and UI is very bad. also, the website is even worse since most of the options are not there at all.

Warning ⚠️ I bought 15 shares ranging from ₹1 to ₹25 and when I sold them they charged DP Charges of ₹14+18% GST on every Share I sold. My wallet balance got in Negative. The problem is that They won't tell you all this before adding funds in your Demat wallet. Everything is hidden beneath FAQ pages if you dig. Invest in Mutual funds to avoid all hidden charges.

weekly_pulse.note: User feedback over the past 10 weeks indicates significant challenges with customer support, with many users reporting unresolved issues and difficulty reaching human agents. The app's user experience is also a concern, with complaints about a cluttered interface, complex navigation, and missing features. Users are also expressing frustration over perceived hidden charges and brokerage fees, particularly for US stock investments. While some positive feedback exists regarding data analysis and US stock investment capabilities, these are overshadowed by critical issues impacting user trust and satisfaction. Addressing these core pain points is crucial for improving retention and overall app perception.
actions: Implement a tiered customer support system with clear escalation paths to human agents for complex issues, and ensure timely callbacks.
Conduct a UX audit focusing on simplifying the interface, improving navigation, and addressing font size and clutter concerns, benchmarking against competitors like Zerodha and Groww.
Review and clearly communicate all charges and fees associated with US stock trading and other transactions, ensuring transparency upfront within the app and on the website.

fee_scenario: Mutual Fund Exit Load on INDmoney Platform
explanation_bullets: An exit load is a charge levied by a mutual fund house when an investor redeems their units before a specified period.
The exit load percentage and the lock-in period for its applicability are determined by the Asset Management Company (AMC) of the specific mutual fund scheme.
INDmoney, as a platform, facilitates the redemption process and displays the applicable exit load as per the fund's scheme information document.
If units are redeemed within the stipulated exit load period, the calculated exit load amount will be deducted from the redemption proceeds.
The exit load is typically a percentage of the Net Asset Value (NAV) at the time of redemption.
